species.citation: (Blume) A.Juss. , Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 2nd Sér. 13 (1840) 266 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 380; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1955) 127; ≡ Hiraea elliptica Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 225.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, ?China, Indochina, Java, Philippines, Sumatra. BILIRAN, BOHOL (photos), CATANDUANES, LUZON: Albay, Bulacan, Cagayan, Laguna, NCR, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Surigao, MINDORO, PANAY, SAMAR, TICAO.

species.citation: (Blume) A.Juss. , Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 2nd Sér. 13 (1840) 267; ≡ Hiraea tomentosa Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 225. var. longirostris (Arènes) Jacobs, Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 5 (1954) 130; ≡ Aspidopterys longirostris Arènes, Notul. Syst. (Paris) 11 (1943) 75; Type: Marche 17 (L, iso), probably from Palawan: April 1884. Distribution: ?Borneo, Philippines. PALAWAN. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, ?Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam. PALAWAN.

species.citation: Hutch. , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1917 (1917) 94. = Aspidopterys glabriuscula var. subrotunda Nied. , Arbeiten Bot. Inst. Königl. Lyceums Hosianum Braunsberg 6 (1915) 13.
species.notes: Record for the Philippines said to be based on Warburg 13393 (Arayat). Not seen by Jacobs for his revision of the family for Flora Malesiana.

species.citation: Bartl. , Linnaea 13 (1839) 552 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1955) 144. ≠ Galphimia glauca auct. non Cav. ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 277. ≠ Thryallis glauca auct. non (Cav.) Kuntze ; Merr., EPFP 2 (1923) 383.
species.notes: Rather widely cultivated as an ornamental in lowlands below 1000m. Not naturalized?

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 March 2023

species.citation: (L.) Kurz , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 43 (1874) 136 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1955) 132; ≡ Banisteria benghalensis L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 427. = Hiptage macroptera Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 189 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 382. = Hiptage pinnata Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1810 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 382. = ?Hiptage tetraptera Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 188 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 382.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan. CEBU (photos), DANJUGAN (photos), LUZON: Laguna, Rizal, MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur, PALAWAN, SIASI.
species.notes: Cultivated in Malesia and in some other tropical countries because of its pretty, fragrant flowers. 0-1000(-1500)m.

species.citation: Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 33 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 381; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1955) 135; Tan et al., PhytoKeys 135 (2019). = Hiptage cebuensis Elmer , LPB 2 (1910) 683 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 381. = Hiptage cumingii Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 190 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 381. = ?Hiptage loheri Merr. , Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 213 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 381. = Hiptage reticulata Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 186 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 381. = ?Hiptage curranii Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 188 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 381. ≠ Hiptage javanica auct. non Blume ; Merr., PJS 5 c (1910) Bot. 190; EPFP 2 (1923) 381.
species.distribution: Philippines, Sulawesi. CEBU, GUIMARAS, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Batangas, Cagayan, Camarines, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Lanao del Sur, Maguindanao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PAMALICAN, PANAY, ROMBLON, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, TICAO.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 187 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 382; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1955) 136. = Hiptage pubescens var. lanceolata Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 187 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 382.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BILIRAN, LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, Rizal (photos), Zambales (photos), PANIQUIAN.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 426 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 5 (1955) 145.
species.notes: Widespread in cultivation as an ornamental. Not naturalized?

species.distribution: Australia, Japan, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malaysia, ?New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Taiwan. BASILAN, BATAN, LUZON: Aurora, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PANAY, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TAWI-TAWI.
